summary: In Eisenmenger syndrome, one true aspect is dilatation of the central pulmonary artery. The peripheral pulmonary arteries show a pattern known as 'pruning,' where smaller vessels become less prominent due to hypoxia-induced vasoconstriction or obliteration. Central structures like right ventricular outflow tract may also enlarge but this isn't universally consistent across all cases; thus it cannot always be considered definitive for every patient with ESMRNSYNDROME.
